mission.title: fluent-bit: out_cloudwatch_logs: log_group_class option introduced breaking change in 3.0.7
mission.description: out_cloudwatch_logs: log_group_class option introduced breaking change in 3.0.7. This issue affects 10+ users.

steps.title: Identify fluent-bit upgrade symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your fluent-bit deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit -n fluent-bit --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review fluent-bit configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant fluent-bit configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl get configmap -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluent-bit
```
## Bug Report

Release 3.0.7 delivered the new log_group_class option support for cloudwatch output, but this introduces a breaking change, causing fluentbit not able to start.

steps.title: Apply the fix for out_cloudwatch_logs: log_group_class option introduced…
steps.description:
Since release 3.0.7 `auto_create_group` couldn't be set to Off anymore. That was a breaking change.

I didn't see any reason why `log_group_class_type` and `log_group_class` should be set, as they're not used when `[auto_]create_group` is off.

Please review  

----
Enter `[N/A]` in the box, if an
```yaml
[2024/06/12 00:07:27] [error] [output:cloudwatch_logs:cloudwatch_logs.0] Configuring log_group_class requires `auto_create_group On`.
```

steps.title: Confirm out_cloudwatch_logs: log_group_class option… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit -n fluent-bit --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n fluent-bit --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Since release 3.0.7 `auto_create_group` couldn't be set to Off anymore. That was a breaking change.

I didn't see any reason why `log_group_class_type` and `log_group_class` should be set, as they're not used when `[auto_]create_group` is off.

Please review  

----
Enter `[N/A]` in the box, if an item is not applicable to your change.
codeSnippets:
[OUTPUT]
    Name cloudwatch_logs
    Match *
    region us-west-2
    log_group_name XXX
    log_stream_prefix XXX
    auto_create_group Off

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with fluent-bit installed or the issue environment reproducible.
